_meta.what: THE REGISTRY for knowledge/addon-dev/ ‚Äî every question about the WoW client that a lab addon could answer, keyed by a stable id.
_meta.why_here: knowledge/addon-dev/ is firewalled from the game KB (its README ¬ß0), and 64 of its 68 `@verify-ingame` markers are backticked, so `wowkb.gen_verify` reports zero addon-dev items. That is a DECISION, not a bug ‚Äî see addon-dev/README.md ¬ß6 and gen_verify.py's docstring. This file is the registry instead.
_meta.id_is_the_key: The join column between this file, the `id` on each ns.Test{} in ClientLab/T_*.lua, and the on-disk run under ClientLabDB.runs. An id survives the anchor line moving; anchor text does not.
_meta.expect_never_ships: `expect` lives HERE and is never compared in-game. cdmp asserts a known answer against regression; the lab discovers an unknown one, so the reader renders result beside expect and a HUMAN decides. An automatic PASS/FAIL would be the instrument grading its own subject.

status.built: a ns.Test{} with this id exists in ClientLab/ (pass 1)
status.deferred: a real, addon-answerable question; not built in pass 1
status.deferred-w1d: tests the addon CONTAINER (.toc / load order), so the lab cannot test it in its own .toc without breaking itself ‚Äî needs a generator that writes sibling addons per test (W1d, not yet designed)
status.candidate: an addon-testable question found under a bare [gap] with no marker; harvested, not built
status.not-answerable: no addon can settle this; the reason is recorded so it is not re-attempted

_meta.cross_check: `wowkb.lab deploy` asserts every `built` id here has a ns.Test{} and every ns.Test{} id is here. An unmatched id is a loud error, never a silent skip.
_meta.counts_as_of: 2026-07-23: 29 built (15 call-and-record + 14 secret-op rows), 20 deferred, 7 deferred-w1d, 11 candidate, 4 not-answerable
_meta.anchor_form: <file under knowledge/addon-dev/>:<line>. `also` lists further anchors that ask the same question ‚Äî one test, several places in the KB to edit when it is answered.

questions.question: Is a nested-`.toc` library (LibStub) enumerated by the client as an addon in its own right? And is a plainly-installed top-level addon (RaiderIO) reported as existing even though it is missing from AddOns.txt?
questions.expect: DoesAddOnExist("LibStub") == false and DoesAddOnExist("RaiderIO") == true. That combination confirms the ¬ß19b folder-name rule AND kills the draft's inference that absence from AddOns.txt proves never-enumerated. Either half coming back the other way rewrites ¬ß1.

questions.question: Does the addon sandbox expose `require`, `os` or `io`?
questions.expect: All three nil. 2298 shipped .lua files contain zero uses of require/dofile/loadfile/module/os./io., which is corroboration by absence; this converts it to a measurement. A non-nil `os` would be a notable finding worth its own KB section.

questions.question: Is `string.rtgsub` ‚Äî wiki-tagged `framexml`, i.e. flagged Blizzard-internal rather than addon-facing ‚Äî actually callable from addon code?
questions.expect: Unknown; that is the point. If the `framexml` tag means anything enforceable, the lookup is nil or the call errors from an insecure frame. If it is merely documentation of intent, it is a normal function and works.

questions.question: Do `table.freeze`, `table.isfrozen`, `table.removemulti` and `strsplittable` exist at 12.0.7? All four are wiki-listed with ZERO uses in the shipped source, so they rest on Tier 2 alone.
questions.expect: All four present as functions. If any is nil, the wiki list is stale and ¬ß5's WoW-additions table needs a correction ‚Äî which is exactly the failure mode a zero-use, Tier-2-only claim has.

questions.question: Does `issecure()` ever return true in an addon call frame ‚Äî i.e. can an addon satisfy the `assert(issecure())` guarding `AddLuaErrorHandler`?
questions.expect: false, from a plain addon call frame. Rule 23 currently states the consequence ('no addon may register a Lua error handler this way') as inference from the assert; this measures the premise. Also probe whether AddLuaErrorHandler is even visible to us.

questions.question: Do the six `C_CombatLog.*` names that are documented only on `C_CombatLogSecure` (AddEventFilter, ClearEventFilters, GetCurrentEntryInfo, GetCurrentEventInfo, GetEntryCount, ShouldShowCurrentEntry) resolve to nil for an addon?
questions.expect: All six nil on `C_CombatLog` for us ‚Äî the `Environment = "SecureOnly"` reading. Any that is non-nil means SecureOnly is not enforced by nil-ing the binding, and ¬ß3's account of the combat-log surface changes.

questions.question: Does `C_EventUtils.IsCallbackEvent` return true for exactly the 12 `CallbackEvent = true` events? Call it on a flagged event and on a non-flagged one.
questions.expect: true for a flagged event (e.g. MINIMAP_PING), false for an unflagged one (e.g. PLAYER_LOGIN). That would upgrade 'the 12 flagged events are exactly what RegisterEventCallback accepts' from a reasonable reading to a measurement. The function is called nowhere in the shipped Lua, so its behaviour is entirely unexercised.

questions.question: Does `Frame:UnregisterEventCallback` exist, and what is its arity? It has exactly one call site in the whole checkout (FrameUtil.lua:59), no generated doc entry, no wiki page, and is absent from the 12.0.0 added-widget list.
questions.expect: Present as a function on a frame (FrameUtil calls it, so it must be, unless FrameUtil path is dead). Existence is the cheap half; arity needs a call and is recorded as whatever the error says.

questions.question: Do `GetCurrentEventID()` and `GetEventTime(eventProfileIndex)` exist and return anything useful when called from an addon? Neither is called anywhere in the 2298 shipped .lua files, so there is no worked example of obtaining a valid eventProfileIndex.
questions.expect: Both exist (they are in FrameScriptDocumentation). GetCurrentEventID called outside a handler probably returns nil or 0; GetEventTime with that value probably errors or returns nothing. Recording the error IS the answer ‚Äî it is what tells a future reader the pair is unusable without a profiling CVar.

questions.question: Is `RegisterUnitEvent`'s documented four-unit cap real? The generated docs express the unit parameter as an unbounded variadic; the four-unit limit is Tier 2 only.
questions.expect: Passing 5 units errors, or silently ignores units past the fourth. Distinguish the two: after registering with 5, the test records whether the call raised. If it neither errors nor is observably capped, the Tier-2 cap claim should be softened in ¬ß2.2.

questions.question: What does the `SecureHooksAllowed = false` annotation actually do? `hooksecurefunc` a function carrying it (`CreateFromMixins`, `Mixin`) and see whether the hook is refused.
questions.expect: Unknown and genuinely open. The annotation appears in 24 generated doc entries and in zero Lua logic anywhere in the shipped source, and it overlaps the wiki's empirically-derived unhookable list on only ONE name ‚Äî which is a reason for caution, not confidence. If the hook installs without error, the natural reading ('hooking these raises') is wrong and both ¬ß2.4 and module-architecture rule 6 need rewriting.

questions.question: Do the deprecated `UIDropDownMenu_*` globals still resolve for addon code at 12.0.7? UIDropDownMenu.lua opens with `local envTable = GetCurrentEnvironment()` and resolves through `envTable[...]` rather than `_G`.
questions.expect: This is the question `ns.GlobalType` exists for: a name may be absent from `_G` yet present in the addon environment, or vice versa. If `_G.UIDropDownMenu_Initialize` is nil, the file is scoped away from addons and LibUIDropDownMenu's continued use by WeakAurasOptions in 2026 gets a causal explanation the KB currently declines to assert.

questions.question: Does `CreateFromMixins(nil)` error, or silently produce an empty mixin? This is what happens if a .toc lists a mixin file after its consumer.
questions.expect: Errors. The Lua SecureMixin implementation does `for k, v in pairs(mixin)`, which raises on nil ‚Äî but Mixin/CreateFromMixins are engine functions whose failure mode has never been observed, so the Lua reading may not describe the C one. A silent empty table would be a much nastier failure mode and worth calling out in ¬ß1.2.

questions.question: Which of the security primitives are actually visible to an addon? `issecure`, `issecurevariable`, `issecretvalue`, `issecrettable`, `canaccessvalue`, `secureexecuterange`, `forceinsecure`, `hooksecurefunc`, `C_Secrets`. All are absent from the generated docs, and `issecurevariable` appears nowhere in the shipped source either ‚Äî the wiki is its only source at any tier.
questions.expect: All present as functions (CDMProbe already calls issecretvalue/issecrettable/canaccessvalue successfully). `issecurevariable` is the one to watch: a single Tier-2 source with zero shipped call sites is exactly the shape of a claim that turns out to be stale. This test is also the PRECONDITION for the whole ¬ß4.2 table below ‚Äî if issecretvalue is missing, Secret.lua cannot gate anything.

questions.question: Is the 16-character addon-message prefix limit real, and how is it enforced? Register a 16-char prefix and a 17-char prefix and record both return values.
questions.expect: 16 succeeds, 17 returns false (or errors). The generated docs type the parameter as a bare `cstring` with no limit; wiki (Tier 2) + AceComm's tested comment (Tier 3) agree on 16, which is the strongest corroboration available and still not Tier 1. Harvested from a bare [gap] with no marker ‚Äî see _meta.status.candidate ‚Äî and promoted to built because it is pure call-and-record.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 1 ‚Äî store a secret in a local / upvalue / table VALUE.
questions.expect: Allowed. If this errors, nothing else in the table is testable and the whole secret-handling chapter is wrong.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 2 ‚Äî pass a secret to a Lua function.
questions.expect: Allowed. This is the premise of every 'launder it through a helper' pattern.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 3 ‚Äî pass a secret to an unmarked C function (e.g. `strlen`-ish builtin / a C API not marked as secret-accepting).
questions.expect: Errors, unless the API is explicitly marked (¬ß4.5). Which C function is chosen matters: the test uses one with no marking so a pass would be genuinely surprising.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 4 ‚Äî concatenate a secret string/number with `..`.
questions.expect: Allowed, and the RESULT is itself secret. The table says allowed but says nothing about the result's secrecy ‚Äî record `issecretvalue` on the result, which is the half the KB does not state.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 5 ‚Äî `string.format` / `string.join` / `string.concat` with a secret argument.
questions.expect: Allowed, result secret. Same as above: the secrecy of the OUTPUT is the interesting half, since it decides whether a formatted string can be printed.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 6 ‚Äî arithmetic on a secret number.
questions.expect: Errors. This is the row CDMProbe's HUD is designed around ‚Äî no cooldown math on a secret.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 7 ‚Äî compare a secret with `==` and with `<`.
questions.expect: Errors. Test BOTH operators separately: `==` on differing types is a plausible engine special case and the table does not distinguish them.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 8 ‚Äî boolean test (`if v then`) on a BOOLEAN secret.
questions.expect: Errors. Needs a secret whose real type is boolean, which the cooldown source may not provide ‚Äî if so this records `skipped` with that reason, never a value.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 9 ‚Äî boolean test on a NON-boolean secret.
questions.expect: Allowed ‚Äî type isn't secret, so nil‚Üífalse and everything else‚Üítrue. The asymmetry with row 8 is the single subtlest rule in the chapter and the one most likely to be misremembered.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 10 ‚Äî length operator `#` on a secret.
questions.expect: Errors.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 11 ‚Äî use a secret as a table KEY.
questions.expect: Errors. Contrast with row 1 (as a table VALUE, allowed) ‚Äî the key/value asymmetry is the rule that bites caching code.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 12 ‚Äî index and index-assign a secret (`secret.foo`, `secret["foo"] = 1`).
questions.expect: Both error. Test read and write separately.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 13 ‚Äî call a secret as a function.
questions.expect: Errors.

questions.question: ¬ß4.2 row 14 ‚Äî `type(secret)`.
questions.expect: Allowed and returns the REAL type. This is ¬ß4.3's Trap 1: type() passes, then the comparison blows up. Record the returned string ‚Äî it also tells us what kind of secret the cooldown source hands out.

questions.question: What happens when a Secret Value reaches AceSerializer-3.0 / LibSerialize / LibDeflate? None of the three tests for secrets; AceSerializer's type dispatch ends in `error(MAJOR..": Cannot serialize a value of type '"..t.."'")`.
questions.expect: AceSerializer sees the real type via `type()` (row 14) and takes a normal branch, then errors inside it on a concat/format/length ‚Äî so the observed failure is a confusing message from the wrong place. Deferred from pass 1 because it needs the three libraries VENDORED into the lab; the ¬ß4.2 rows measure the underlying operations first, which is most of the answer.

questions.question: Can `RegisterAllEvents` be combined with `UnregisterEvent`? The wiki says no; Blizzard's own Event Trace does exactly that (`self:RegisterAllEvents()` at :107, `self:UnregisterEvent("ADDON_LOADED")` at :702). One of the two is wrong.
questions.expect: Needs a scratch frame plus observation over time ‚Äî register all, unregister one, count arrivals of that one. Bucket 2.

questions.question: What do the `SynchronousEvent` / `unique` flags mean? Neither string is documented anywhere reachable ‚Äî not in the generated docs' Documentation field, not in Blizzard_APIDocumentation, not in Blizzard_EventTrace, and the wiki has no page.
questions.expect: The natural reading (synchronous = dispatched inline; non-synchronous = queued; unique = coalesced to at most one queued instance) is inference, not evidence. Testing it needs firing an event from a known call frame and timestamping the handler ‚Äî bucket 2, and it may not be reachable from an addon at all.

questions.question: Is `OnUpdate` really blocked by hiding a frame or its parent?
questions.expect: Tier 2 and dated; oUF guards on `self:IsVisible()` independently, which is consistent but not proof. Scratch frame + tick counter across a Hide/Show cycle.

questions.question: Is the leading `_nilOwner` argument to a RegisterEventCallback handler a contract, or six consistent conventions? Is it ever non-nil?
questions.expect: The generated docs declare `EventCallbackType` with NO fields at all, so there is no spec to read. Six shipped call sites all discard a leading arg. Needs registering a callback and inspecting arg 1.

questions.question: Does `ADDONS_UNLOADING` carry `closingClient = false` on a /reload?
questions.expect: Tier 1 supplies only the payload shape; that a reload passes false is inference FROM THE FIELD NAME. Needs a logout/reload observation recorded into SavedVariables ‚Äî pass 3's journal.

questions.question: What state does a /reload preserve vs discard ‚Äî is the Lua state fully destroyed or recycled?
questions.expect: No Tier-1 or Tier-2 statement exists. Behaviourally addon chunks re-execute (ADDON_LOADED fires again). Testable by stamping a non-saved global and looking for it after reload ‚Äî pass 3's journal.

questions.question: What is the real order of PLAYER_LEAVING_WORLD ‚Üí PLAYER_LOGOUT ‚Üí ADDONS_UNLOADING, and does it differ on a crash/Alt-F4?
questions.expect: The wiki carries its own {{fact}} tags on both bullets, i.e. it does not vouch for the ordering. Needs the pass-3 journal (record each event with an incrementing sequence number, read it back next session). The crash half is not testable politely.

questions.question: Does `CreateFontString` accept a fourth `subLevel` argument, or is it silently discarded? Blizzard's own pool code passes four against a documented three-arg signature.
questions.expect: Create two FontStrings on the same frame and draw layer with different subLevel values and see whether stacking order differs. Bucket 2.

questions.question: What does frameStrata `PARENT` actually do? It is the XSD default and is never written explicitly anywhere in the shipped corpus.
questions.expect: Tier 1 gives the value and the default and nothing more. The dropped gloss ('inherit from parent, not a z-band') was inference from the name.

questions.question: Do frame levels run 0‚Äì10000, does a child default to one level above its parent, and does moving a parent below 0 clamp to 10000?
questions.expect: All three are Tier 2 only. Cheap once a scratch frame exists ‚Äî create parent/child, read GetFrameLevel, then SetFrameLevel(0) on the parent and re-read.

questions.question: Is the XSD's listing order of the five draw layers actually the z-order, and does HIGHLIGHT have special mouse-over semantics?
questions.expect: Universally believed, matches the names, and stated at Tier 1 nowhere. Needs two overlapping textures and a visual read ‚Äî the one pass-1 bucket that a SavedVariables record cannot capture on its own.

questions.question: Are SetTexture / SetAtlas / SetColorTexture mutually exclusive ‚Äî does exactly one 'win'? `SetTexture(...)` then `SetAtlas(...)`, read GetTexture()/GetAtlas(), then the reverse order.
questions.expect: The near-universal mental model, consistent with GetTexture returning nil after SetAtlas, and cited at no tier.

questions.question: Do SetVertexColor and SetGradient write the same storage? White 8x8 texture, SetVertexColor(1,0,0), SetGradient(HORIZONTAL, yellow, blue), GetVertexColor(); then reverse.
questions.expect: README ¬ß6 calls this the most consequential unresolved gap in the whole subtree, and the workspace has already observed it once ‚Äî todo/addon-engineering.md records that SetGradient RESETS the vertex colour to white. That prior observation makes this a confirm-and-cite, not a discovery.

questions.question: Do FontString:SetTextColor and Region:GetVertexColor share storage? `fs:SetTextColor(1,0,0)` then `fs:GetVertexColor()`, and the reverse.
questions.expect: 'Identical annotations ‚áí identical storage' is inference. The two wiki pages say nothing about the relationship, and one of them was effectively abandoned in 2021.

questions.question: What does an alpha or vertex-colour animation do to the underlying value when it stops WITHOUT `setToFinalAlpha`?
questions.expect: The attribute's existence implies restoration, but that is inference and is stated at no tier. Needs an AnimationGroup and a read after OnFinished.

questions.question: Does an XML `<OnLoad>` body receive the addon-table varargs? Of 2123 shipped `<OnLoad` occurrences, 6 have `...` nearby and all six forward event/callback varargs ‚Äî but that is four distinct sites, one of them dead code.
questions.expect: An argument from silence, not evidence; the KB explicitly declines to assume either way. Needs the lab to ship an XML file.

questions.question: Can an addon declare an `intrinsic` in XML and then use it as a `CreateFrame` frameType? The XSD puts `intrinsic` in the shared FrameAttributes group, so an addon CAN write it.
questions.expect: No Tier-1/Tier-2 statement that it becomes usable, and zero of the seven surveyed addon clones do it. Needs the lab to ship an XML file.

questions.question: May an addon use `<ScopedModifier>` (forbidden / scriptsUseGivenEnv / addToSecureEnv / hideFromGlobalEnv / fullLockdown), or is it silently ignored?
questions.expect: In UI.xsd (Tier 1), used by 20 Blizzard XML files and by 0 of the surveyed addons and 0 third-party XML files on the live install. Needs the lab to ship an XML file.

questions.question: Does an XML `<Color>` element compile to SetVertexColor or to SetColorTexture? Load a texture from XML with both `file` and `<Color>`, then read GetTexture() and GetVertexColor().
questions.expect: Not stated by UI.xsd or the wiki. The evidence rules out plain SetColorTexture replacement but does not distinguish the two remaining readings. Needs the lab to ship an XML file.

questions.question: Does an absent `## OptionalDeps` entry block the load? The wiki says OptionalDeps names addons that 'should load first if available' but does NOT say an absent one is harmless.
questions.expect: Harmless ‚Äî the natural reading of 'if available', with no counter-example in the shipped corpus, held as [unverified]. Needs a generated sibling addon naming a nonexistent optional dep.

questions.question: Does a Lua error inside one .toc-listed file abort only that file, or the whole addon's remaining files?
questions.expect: Not established at any tier ‚Äî the generated docs give only DoesAddOnHaveLoadError with no semantics, and both wiki pages are silent. Needs a sibling addon with a deliberately broken middle file and a marker global set by the last file.

questions.question: Is the client's login-time loader transitive over dependencies? Blizzard's Lua demand-load helper walks them recursively, but that proves only the HELPER is transitive.
questions.expect: No Tier-1 or Tier-2 statement of engine-level transitivity found. Needs a three-addon chain A‚ÜíB‚ÜíC.

questions.question: May a third-party addon use `## LoadIntoEnvironment` / `## AllowLoadEnvironment`? Both are observed in Blizzard .toc files and absent from the wiki's TOC format page, with 0 uses across the install's 147 third-party .toc files.
questions.expect: Treated as Blizzard-internal until proven otherwise. Needs a sibling addon setting the tag.

questions.question: Is a third-party `## Secure` ignored, honoured, or rejected? 5 shipped Blizzard tocs set it, 0 of 147 third-party ones do, and the wiki does not document the tag at all ‚Äî it is undocumented, NOT documented-as-restricted.
questions.expect: Unknown; that is the finding to make. Needs a sibling addon setting it.

questions.question: Is the .toc directive parser case-insensitive?
questions.expect: Not established. Needs a sibling addon with `## interface:` / `## SAVEDVARIABLES:` in odd casing.

questions.question: Are .toc files loaded strictly top-to-bottom? The rule is Tier 2 only, corroborated only by Blizzard's own tocs being internally consistent with it.
questions.expect: True. Trivially testable with a sibling addon whose files append their own name to a table ‚Äî and worth doing precisely because everything in the module-architecture chapter rests on it.

questions.question: WHY is a string accepted where an enum is declared ‚Äî does the C layer map the token string onto the enum, or does the doc generator merely label unit-token parameters with that type name?
questions.expect: NOT ANSWERABLE from Lua. Both readings produce identical observable behaviour, so no call can distinguish them; only Blizzard source or a statement could. The KB's handling is already right: use the string form (that is what ships), assert no mechanism.

questions.question: What does `taintLog` level 5 (added 12.0.1, per the wiki) log? The BlizzardInterfaceResources CVar description stops at level 4.
questions.expect: NOT ANSWERABLE BY AN ADDON. Needs `/console taintLog 1`, a full client restart, and then reading `_retail_/Logs/taint.log` OFF DISK ‚Äî three things outside a Lua addon's reach. Answerable by a human plus a file read, and worth doing that way; just not by ClientLab.

questions.question: When a secret is written to SavedVariables, does the file really get 'a comment' alongside the nil?
questions.expect: The COMMENT half is not answerable by an addon ‚Äî it needs reading the written file off disk, which is a wowkb job, not a Lua one. The other half ‚Äî 'secrets become nil' ‚Äî IS addon-testable via a two-session round trip (write a secret to the DB, /reload, read it back), and is recorded as `savedvars-secret-roundtrip` below.

questions.question: Does a Secret Value stored in SavedVariables come back as nil after a /reload?
questions.expect: Traceable only to a Discord blue post archived on the wiki; the KB could not produce it locally because no SavedVariable on this install held a secret. A two-session round trip settles it. PASS 3 ‚Äî it is the first test that needs the journal (write in session N, assert in session N+1), so it validates that machinery.

questions.question: How does the SavedVariables writer format numbers ‚Äî what precision, and what does it do with NaN / inf?
questions.expect: Unverified at every tier; the writer is C, not shipped Lua, so ¬ß1.5 is entirely inferred from output files. Write known values, /reload, read back.

questions.question: What does the SavedVariables writer do with a table cycle, and is there a depth limit?
questions.expect: Unverified at every tier. Risky to test ‚Äî a hang or a corrupt file is a plausible outcome ‚Äî so it wants its own throwaway SavedVariable, not ClientLabDB.

questions.question: Is SavedVariables key ordering stable across writes?
questions.expect: Unverified. Matters for anything diffing the file between sessions ‚Äî which wowkb.cdmp and wowkb.lab both do.

questions.question: Is the `.bak` file a rename or a copy, and when is it written relative to the main file?
questions.expect: Undocumented at Tier 1 and Tier 2; WoWUIBugs #241 is Tier-2 evidence it is not a reliable recovery mechanism. Half of this is a disk observation, not an addon one.

questions.question: Is the 255-byte addon-message body limit real, and what happens on overflow ‚Äî truncation, rejection, or an error?
questions.expect: Same Tier-2/Tier-3-only footing as the prefix limit. Deferred past the prefix test because sending actually puts traffic on a channel, which the prefix registration does not.

questions.question: Are the addon-message throttle numbers (10 allowance, 1/s regen) real?
questions.expect: Tier 2 only, and the same source says the server may change them at any time ‚Äî the KB's own instruction is DO NOT ENCODE THEM. Measuring them would produce a number with a shelf life, so this is recorded mainly so a future session does not think it was missed.

questions.question: Are there length or rate limits on `C_BattleNet.SendGameData`?
questions.expect: No limits documented at any tier. Testing needs a second Battle.net-connected client, which makes it much more expensive than it looks.

questions.question: Under what conditions do the six `MayReturnNothing` functions of `C_EncodingUtil` return nothing?
questions.expect: Nothing states the conditions. Pure call-and-record once someone enumerates plausible bad inputs ‚Äî a good pass-2 addition, cheap and self-contained.

questions.question: In what order do two frames registered for the same event receive it ‚Äî registration order, creation order, or unspecified?
questions.expect: No Tier-1 statement, and the wiki's account is explicitly disclaimed by the wiki itself. Two scratch frames plus a shared counter. Bucket 2.

questions.question: Under `## LoadSavedVariablesFirst`, when do ADDON_LOADED and SAVED_VARIABLES_TOO_LARGE fire relative to the .toc's last file?
questions.expect: The only statement is a PROPOSAL in a feature request, not a shipped-behaviour statement. Needs the lab's own .toc to set the tag, which changes the lab's own load behaviour ‚Äî so it belongs with the W1d container work rather than with the in-process tests.
